creator: empty.japan
caption:
By “new” I mean new for me - the house is actually over 20 years old 😆 Obviously, I was looking for something a bit more fresh, but it is literally impossible to get something new if you have 2 dogs 😅

I still cannot believe how lucky I was with this house, which, by the way, was the only decent pet-friendly option on the market.

Here is what I got:
📍 Monthly rent - USD 570 + utilities
📍 180 sq m + garage - yep, it’s huge, and I only use the first floor 😆 I would gladly rent something smaller if there were other options.
📍 It takes 40 minutes by car to get to the center of Nagano, which is perfect since my upper limit was 1 hour. Another important point for me is that it takes exactly 2 hours “door to door” to get from my house to Tokyo station, which is even faster than when I lived in Kyoto.
📍 It’s really hard to get around without a car. The bus to the closest train station only runs twice a day, but that’s why the rent is relatively low.
📍 One of the most welcoming and kindest neighbors and local communities I have ever experienced in Japan. There were a lot of questions from people worried about how locals treat foreigners - honestly, I have never had any bad experiences.
📍 Tons of amazing ski resorts, from some of the most popular ones to hidden gems without any crowds.
📍 The sea is just a 1-hour ride away, where you can enjoy really nice beaches in the summer and some of the best seafood all year round, which I am fully taking advantage of 😋
📍 An abundance of hot springs that I visit almost weekly.
📍 The area where I live is surrounded by mountains, and you can find some amazing hikes that feel like Lord of the Rings adventures within 30 minutes, ranging from day hikes to multiple-day ones.
📍 Last but definitely not least - FOOD. Absolutely delicious restaurants and fresh products straight from nearby farms. I feel like it’s 1.5 to 2 times cheaper than in Kyoto.

What do you think?
Would you settle down with something like this?

Honestly, for me, this is a dream come true! I could’ve never found such a place if it wasn’t for this house, and I am already looking for something more permanent in the area 🤞
